description_long: This candle is sculpted in the form of a grotesque anglerfish, featuring a bulbous, mottled brown body with reddish streaks and a prominent, curved lure extending from its head. The front displays a wide, gaping mouth filled with exaggerated, jagged white teeth and two bulging eyes, giving it a menacing appearance. The surface is textured to resemble rough, organic skin, with subtle variations in color and visible creases. The tail is short and tapers to a point, while the lure is semi-translucent and pale, mimicking the bioluminescent appendage of a real anglerfish. There is no visible flame or wax drips, and the wick is faintly visible at the tip of the lure. The candle does not appear to have a separate holder or base, suggesting it is designed to rest directly on a flat surface.
description: A grotesque anglerfish-shaped candle with a mottled brown body, jagged white teeth, and a curved lure extending from its head. The surface is rough and organic, with no visible holder or wax drips.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the pointed tail and the curved lure extending from the top, with the body’s mottled texture visible.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the anglerfish’s curved lure, bulging eye, and the wide mouth with jagged teeth.
description_view_2: The front view displays the gaping mouth filled with sharp white teeth, two bulging eyes, and the lure extending from the forehead.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the lure, eye, and the organic, streaked body texture.
